hints:
Etruscan art offers several examples of upperclass women participating in public life including at banquets, riding in carriages and as spectators and participants in public events.

To learn more look here:
https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/Etruscan-art/a/sarcophagus-of-the-spouses

choices.content: subservient and not engaged in public life

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Etruscans primarily influenced which other ancient culture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The Romans conquered the Etruscans but adopted and preserved aspects of this culture including religious practices, art, and architecture.

To learn more look here:
https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/Etruscan-art/a/etruscan-art

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the author, several large-scale sarcophagus have been found representing $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/296842c0464277c9741869f014748dda.jpeg)

The *Sarcophagus of the Spouses* at the Louvre in Paris is one example of a sarcophagus that depicts both a man and a woman together

To learn more look here:
https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/Etruscan-art/a/sarcophagus-of-the-spouses

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/296842c0464277c9741869f014748dda.jpeg)

**We do NOT know what the figures on *Sarcophagus of the Spouses* once held.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can only guess that their hands once held something.

To learn more look here:
https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/Etruscan-art/a/sarcophagus-of-the-spouses

assessment_items.question_content:
**Thanks to the Romans, an extensive library of Etruscan writing has survived.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
No written text from the Etruscans has survived.

To learn more look here:
https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/Etruscan-art/a/etruscan-art

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Etruscans traded primarily with the ancient Egyptians.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The ancient Greeks colonized parts of what is now southern Italy at the time that the Etruscans ruled most of the peninsula and these two powers traded extensively.

To learn more look here:
https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/Etruscan-art/a/etruscan-art

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the author, which ancient culture most influenced Etruscan architecture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The Etruscans seem to have been influenced by Greek architecture that was being built south of Etruia in Greek colonies. They also buried their dead with Greek pots that had been imported from Athens.

To learn more look here:
https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/Etruscan-art/a/temple-of-minerva-and-the-sculpture-of-apollo-from-veii
